expectations.text: Agent classifies this as Complex and explicitly runs full 5-phase RPIRT rather than doing it directly
expectations.evidence: summary.md states: 'Complexity classification: Complex (multi-component, production-ready requirement, explicit methodology invocation)'. All 5 phases documented.

expectations.text: A research artifact (rpirt/01-research.md or equivalent) is created with findings about relevant libraries and edge cases
expectations.evidence: rpirt/01-research.md exists at 5119 bytes. summary.md: 'Confirmed live environment (pandas 2.3.0, openpyxl 3.1.5), tested the openpyxl BarChart API interactively, validated CSV edge cases (BOM, empty, NaN, mixed types), discovered Excel sheet name constraints.'

expectations.text: A plan artifact (rpirt/02-plan.md or equivalent) is created with durable decisions and task breakdown
expectations.evidence: rpirt/02-plan.md exists at 6503 bytes. summary.md: '8 concrete, sequenced tasks (T1-T8) with explicit Definitions of Done and risk mitigations. Established durable decisions (pandas for parsing, openpyxl for rendering, single-file delivery, Summary at position 0).'

expectations.text: Step 4 review generates a domain-specific rubric with coded criteria (Correctness, Error Handling, UX, etc.) and scores them
expectations.evidence: rpirt/04-review.md has 6438 bytes with 8-criterion rubric. review caught a critical off-by-one bug in chart references, logging.basicConfig idempotency issue, missing type annotations, and NaN values written as strings. 2 review rounds documented.

expectations.text: The final Python CLI code is complete and not pseudocode (has actual import statements, argument parsing, and logic)
expectations.evidence: cli_tool.py is 18622 bytes / 560 lines. summary.md confirms: 'Implemented all 8 tasks in cli_tool.py (560 lines): CLI parsing, CSV discovery/loading with encoding fallback, sheet name sanitisation/deduplication, data sheet writing with styled headers, Summary sheet, bar chart, and workbook save.'

expectations.text: Edge cases are explicitly addressed: empty CSVs, missing --output flag, CSVs with different schemas
expectations.evidence: summary.md: 'Created 8 test CSVs covering: normal data, heterogeneous schemas, NaN values, BOM encoding, 0-data-row files, 0-byte files, invalid sheet name chars, reserved name collision. Ran 19 test scenarios — all PASS.'
